title: Fix P0: Vehicle profile shows "Loading vehicle..." indefinitely — get_vehicle_profile_data RPC times out
description: CRITICAL: get_vehicle_profile_data RPC (VehicleProfile.tsx line 1754) hits 3-second statement timeout for ALL logged-out users. Every vehicle profile URL (nuke.ag/vehicle/[id]) shows a spinner that never resolves. Confirmed via direct test: POST /rest/v1/rpc/get_vehicle_profile_data with anon key returns HTTP 500 canceling statement due to statement timeout in 3.2s. Fix options: (1) Optimize the RPC — add SECURITY DEFINER, reduce joins, add indexes on vehicles(id) where is_public=true, (2) Skip the RPC for logged-out users and use a lightweight direct query (.select('id,year,make,model,vin,primary_image_url,sale_price,status') not select(*)), (3) Add 4-second client-side timeout on the RPC with immediate fallback to the direct query. File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/VehicleProfile.tsx loadVehicle() function lines 1731-1797. Also check the DB function: SELECT proname, prosrc FROM pg_proc WHERE proname='get_vehicle_profile_data' to see what it is doing. Validated when: nuke.ag/vehicle/6442df03-9cac-43a8-b89e-e4fb4c08ee99 loads vehicle data within 3 seconds without a login.

title: Fix P0: /market shows "Coming Soon" placeholder — MarketDashboard.tsx is a stub, replace with real exchange data
description: CRITICAL: nuke.ag/market routes to MarketDashboard.tsx which is a hardcoded Coming Soon placeholder with fake static stats (800K+ vehicles, 625K+ data points). The real market exchange with live data is at /market/exchange (MarketExchange.tsx) which calls api-v1-exchange returning real fund data in 0.6s. Fix: In nuke_frontend/src/routes/modules/marketplace/routes.tsx, change the root route element from MarketDashboard to MarketExchange: change <Route path="/" element={<MarketDashboard />} /> to <Route path="/" element={<MarketExchange />} />. Also update the /dashboard route. Delete or repurpose MarketDashboard.tsx. The api-v1-exchange?action=funds endpoint returns live PORS (Porsche, $5B market cap), TRUK (Trucks, $1.3B), SQBD (Squarebody, $80M), Y79 (1979, $317M) fund data. File: nuke_frontend/src/routes/modules/marketplace/routes.tsx line 31. Validated when: nuke.ag/market shows ETF fund cards with live NAV data.

title: Fix P0: Homepage Feed tab shows "Unable to load vehicles" error — fix CursorHomepage vehicle query
description: CRITICAL: Clicking Browse Feed on the homepage shows 'Unable to load vehicles. Please try refreshing the page.' Error originates in nuke_frontend/src/pages/CursorHomepage.tsx line 1078 setError call. The query chain runs: selectV2 (canonical_vehicle_type, canonical_body_style columns) -> falls back to selectV1 -> applies listing_kind='vehicle' filter at line 933-935 -> fails. The listing_kind column either does not exist or causes an error. Root cause investigation: (1) Run SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name='vehicles' AND column_name='listing_kind' to confirm if column exists. (2) If it exists, check if listingKindSupportedRef correctly detects support — see line 933: if (listingKindSupportedRef.current) { q = q.eq('listing_kind', 'vehicle') }. The ref may be stuck true even when column errors occur. Fix: Add better error handling in the query loop — if listing_kind filter causes an error, set listingKindSupportedRef.current = false and retry without it. File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/CursorHomepage.tsx lines 900-1081. Validated when: homepage Browse Feed shows vehicle cards.

title: Fix: Search takes 11+ seconds — slow search edge function and no loading indicator
description: The search edge function (/functions/v1/search) takes 11.5 seconds to respond for 'porsche 911'. During this time the Search.tsx page shows static content with no spinner, skeleton, or progress indicator. Users cannot tell if search is working. Also the SearchResults component shows '0 results' and 'NO RESULTS FOUND' immediately while still loading, which is deceptive — users think search failed. Two fixes needed: (1) Performance: In IntelligentSearch.tsx lines 680-697, try calling universal-search instead of search — universal-search returns in 3.8s and returns correct results. Test: supabase.functions.invoke('universal-search', {body: {query, limit: 24}}). (2) UX: While loading is true, SearchResults or Search.tsx should show skeleton cards (8-12 grey placeholder cards in a grid) instead of the NO RESULTS empty state. File: nuke_frontend/src/components/search/IntelligentSearch.tsx line 680, nuke_frontend/src/pages/Search.tsx (loading state at line 50, results render at line 1083). Validated when: search query shows skeleton within 0.5s and results within 5s.

title: Fix: Navigation has no links to Search or Market — add Search+Market to header nav
description: The site header shows only 3 links to all users (logged-out and logged-in): Home (/), + (/capture), Profile (/profile). No link to /search or /market. This is the primary navigation and it hides the two most important pages of the product. The NukeMenu dropdown (data-marque-menu button) likely contains more links but users cannot discover it. Fix: Add Search and Market as visible links in the header. Option 1: Add text links in the header-right area (desktop only). Option 2: Add icons to the existing icon-link row. Option 3: Ensure the NukeMenu dropdown is visually indicated as a menu (e.g., show 'Menu' or a hamburger icon). File: Find and edit the AppHeader component — likely nuke_frontend/src/components/layout/AppHeader.tsx. The nav HTML shows: class='header-wrapper' > class='header-content' > class='header-slot-left' > class='header-left' > data-marque-menu='true' button. Validated when: top header shows a link/button that navigates to /search and another to /market without any additional clicks.

title: Fix: Search empty state shows blank page — add featured vehicles when no query
description: nuke.ag/search with no query shows only a search input box with no content to browse. Users who land on /search have nothing to interact with. The page body shows 'Search workstation & quick links' and footer — nothing else. Fix: When searchQuery is empty (the initial state), fetch and display 12-24 featured vehicles. Use a simple Supabase query: .from('vehicles').select('id,year,make,model,primary_image_url,sale_price').eq('is_public',true).not('primary_image_url','is',null).not('year','is',null).order('sale_price',{ascending:false,nullsFirst:false}).limit(24). Render these using the same VehicleCard components used for search results. Add a label like 'Top Vehicles by Value' or 'Featured'. Also make sure the filter panel is accessible even with no query — right now the filter toggle button may not render when results are empty. File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/Search.tsx around lines 1043-1080 (No-query empty state section). Validated when: /search with no query shows a grid of vehicle cards.

title: Fix: Search filter panel not showing Year/Price/Make filters — vFilters UI not rendering
description: Search.tsx has a complete VehicleFilters state (priceMin, priceMax, yearMin, yearMax, mileageMax, transmission, listingStatus) and filter logic — but the filter UI is not visible to users. Only type filters (Vehicles/Orgs/etc) and Sort are shown. The actual attribute filters are hidden behind a showFilters toggle that does not appear to render the filter inputs. Fix: Find where vFilters.showFilters is checked in the render and ensure the filter panel HTML is rendered. Check if there is a conditional like {vFilters.showFilters && <FilterPanel />} that is never triggering. The Filter button text shows 'Filter:' with type options but no Make/Year/Price fields. File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/Search.tsx — search for showFilters usage in JSX (not in the state definition). Also check nuke_frontend/src/components/search/ for a separate FilterPanel component. The VIN search widget (showVinSearch toggle, state defined at line 73) is also not visible — ensure it renders. Validated when: search page shows Year Min/Max, Price Min/Max, and Make filter inputs.

title: Fix: Investor offering page gate shows no traction data — add live stats to access code screen
description: nuke.ag/offering shows only an access code gate with contact email. Investors who receive this link but have not yet entered a code see ZERO proof of product traction. Fix: Add a stats strip to the InvestorOffering.tsx access-code screen showing live platform stats. Use the db-stats edge function or hardcode real numbers: 1.25M vehicles indexed, 33.7M vehicle images, 513K Nuke AI valuations, 4 market segment ETFs. These numbers are real and compelling. Add them above or below the access code form in a simple 2x2 stat grid. No API call needed — hardcode from the db-stats numbers confirmed today (2026-02-27). File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/InvestorOffering.tsx — find the gate/access-code render section and add a stats strip. Validated when: nuke.ag/offering shows at least 3 numeric stats (vehicles, images, estimates) BEFORE entering the access code.

title: Fix: Search shows 'NO RESULTS FOUND' while loading — loading skeleton needed in SearchResults
description: When search is running (loading=true, results still pending), the page displays the NO RESULTS FOUND empty state immediately. Users see the search fail before it has even completed. This needs a loading skeleton. Fix: In nuke_frontend/src/pages/Search.tsx, when loading=true and searchQuery is not empty, render skeleton card placeholders instead of the empty state. Example: render 12 grey placeholder boxes (100% width/height divs with animate-pulse CSS) in the same grid as VehicleCards. This creates the perception of fast response. The loading state is tracked in useState at line 50. The results render is at line 1083 ({(results.length > 0 || searchQuery) && ...}). Add a check: if loading && searchQuery, show skeletons. File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/Search.tsx and optionally nuke_frontend/src/components/search/SearchResults.tsx. Validated when: after submitting a search query, grey skeleton cards appear within 0.5s.

title: Fix: Navigation missing logo/brand mark — add Nuke wordmark to header
description: The site header has no logo. The brand name 'Nuke' appears only as a dropdown button trigger with a small triangle indicator. Users do not see a clear wordmark or logo. This makes the site look unfinished. Fix: Add a visible 'NUKE' wordmark to the top-left of the header as a link to /. It should be always visible (not inside a dropdown). Can be styled as the existing large font treatment from the homepage hero. File: Find AppHeader.tsx in nuke_frontend/src/components/layout/. The current header HTML shows: data-marque-menu='true' button with text 'Nuke' and a small triangle. Change this to have the wordmark visible at all times as a link, and keep the dropdown for the menu separately. Validated when: every page shows 'NUKE' or the logo in the top-left corner as a visible, permanent element.

title: Fix: Mobile nav (375px) missing Search and Market links — add to bottom nav bar
description: On 375px mobile, the bottom nav bar shows: Home (→/), + (→/capture), Profile (→/profile). Search (/search) and Market (/market) are completely inaccessible on mobile. Fix: Update the mobile bottom navigation to include Search and optionally Market. Change the 3-item nav to: Search (magnifying glass), + (add/capture), Home, Profile. Or expand to 5 items: Home, Search, +, Market, Profile. File: Find the mobile bottom nav component — likely nuke_frontend/src/components/layout/MobileBottomNav.tsx or AppLayout.tsx. Check where the Home/+/Profile bottom bar is rendered. The bottom nav HTML shows items: Home (→/), + (→/capture), Profile (→/profile). Validated when: on 375px screen, a bottom nav tap navigates to /search.

title: Fix: Vehicle profile workspace tab labels are confusing — rename Evidence/Facts/Commerce/Financials
description: The vehicle profile has 4 workspace tabs: Evidence (Timeline/gallery/intake), Facts (AI confidence/valuations), Commerce (Listings/supporters/offers), Financials (Pricing/history/docs). These labels are internal product jargon and confusing to external users. Suggested improvements: rename Facts → Valuation (more intuitive for what the Nuke Estimate and deal score live under), rename Commerce → Listings (clearer), rename Financials → Pricing. Evidence is acceptable but could be renamed to History or Timeline. File: nuke_frontend/src/pages/VehicleProfile.tsx lines 75-80 — the WORKSPACE_TABS constant. Change label fields. No backend changes needed. Also confirm that the Comps/Comparable Sales functionality (SimilarSalesSection.tsx) is reachable from one of these tabs — if it is under Commerce/Listings or Financials/Pricing, the tab label change will help users find it. Validated when: a non-technical person can immediately understand what data lives under each tab.

title: Fix: Similar Sales (Comps) section not visible on vehicle profile — verify SimilarSalesSection is wired to a tab
description: The SimilarSalesSection.tsx component was built (nuke_frontend/src/components/vehicle/SimilarSalesSection.tsx) and the api-v1-comps edge function returns auction comparable data correctly. However a Comps or Comparable Sales section is not visible on the vehicle profile at nuke.ag/vehicle/[id]. Investigate: (1) In VehicleProfile.tsx, search for SimilarSalesSection — is it imported and rendered? (2) Check VehicleComparablesTab.tsx (nuke_frontend/src/pages/vehicle-profile/VehicleComparablesTab.tsx) — is this component used anywhere in VehicleProfile.tsx? (3) Which workspace tab (Evidence/Facts/Commerce/Financials) renders VehicleComparablesTab? The tab IDs are: evidence, facts, commerce, financials. Search for VehicleComparablesTab in VehicleProfile.tsx to find which tab renders it. Fix: Ensure SimilarSalesSection is rendered and receiving vehicleId prop. If the vehicle profile is not loading (see P0 task), this cannot be tested until that is fixed. Validated when: after fixing vehicle profile loading, the Financials or Commerce tab shows Similar Sales with prices and dates.
